title: Build pipeline v0: SVG normalize + style-lint + PNG/sprite/OBF export
context: open-pictograms defines SVG as the single source of truth; PNG rasters, SVG sprites, and OBF exports are all derived. To make this deterministic and enforceable, a build pipeline must exist before any symbols are drawn at scale. The pipeline must automate SVG normalization (via SVGO or equivalent), run the automated style-lint gate (checking grid/viewBox, stroke weight, palette, clean SVG — no raster embeds, no external refs, no stray metadata), and produce all required output formats. Without this, style compliance is aspirational rather than machine-enforced, and the 'drawn symbol' milestone cannot be validated.
objective: Implement and ship version 0 of the build pipeline: SVG normalize → automated style-lint gate → PNG raster export (multiple sizes) → SVG sprite → OBF export for AAC reuse; all as a runnable, documented TypeScript/ESM pnpm workspace package.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG normalization step (using SVGO or equivalent) produces clean, accessible SVGs with <title> and role=img, removing stray metadata, raster embeds, and external refs.

Style-lint gate checks each SVG against the design system: canvas/viewBox dimensions, grid alignment, stroke weight, allowed palette, and clean-SVG rules; a non-conforming SVG exits non-zero and is rejected from further pipeline stages.
PNG export produces at least two standard sizes from SVG source; SVG sprite bundles all passing symbols into a single file.
OBF export produces a valid Open Board Format file containing passing symbols with concept IDs, labels (from metadata), and CC-BY-4.0 attribution.
Pipeline is TypeScript/ESM, runs via pnpm, passes pnpm build && pnpm lint, has no secrets in logs or artifacts, and is licensed MIT.
resources: Open Board Format (OBF) — open AAC board interchange format
output: pipeline/ TypeScript/ESM package in the repo implementing SVG normalize, style-lint, PNG/sprite/OBF export; CI-runnable with pnpm; licensed MIT. PR opened against main with a passing build and lint.
